turns.content: I am BEGGING this city to give me one dry day. just one. thats all I'm asking for wallah
Manchester weather strikes again? What happened?

so I left the house this morning and it was actually sunny right. like blue sky, birds singing, the whole thing. so I thought you know what I dont need an umbrella today. MISTAKE. by the time I got halfway to the tram it was absolutely chucking it down. soaked through in like 3 minutes
The classic Manchester trap — sunny when you leave, sideways rain five minutes later. Did you have to go into work drenched?

YES. walked in looking like I'd just swam there. my colleague was dead like 'did you forget you live in Manchester?' like yeah fair point tbh I should know better by now. I've literally lived here my whole life and I still get caught out
You'd think after a lifetime you'd have an umbrella surgically attached. Is it one of those weeks where it's just been non-stop rain?

it has rained EVERY SINGLE DAY this week. I checked the forecast and it says rain until at least Thursday. it's April!! where is spring?? I saw people in London posting photos in like t-shirts and sunshine and I could actually scream

April in Manchester is basically an extension of winter with slightly warmer rain. London always seems to dodge the worst of it. Are you more annoyed about the weather itself or the getting-caught-without-an-umbrella part?

both tbh but mostly I'm just tired of grey. like it affects your mood init. I wake up and its dark and grey, I leave work and its dark and grey. I havent seen proper sunshine in weeks. my vitamin D is probably in the minus at this point loool

The grey skies definitely take a toll on mood, especially when it drags on for weeks. A lot of people in the UK actually are vitamin D deficient, especially in northern cities. Do you take a supplement? And have you got any plans to escape somewhere sunny anytime soon?

I keep saying I'll book a holiday somewhere warm but then I never actually do it. like my friends keep talking about going to Turkey or Morocco in summer and I'm always like yeah sounds good and then I get too busy and it doesn't happen. maybe this year I need to actually commit

You should genuinely book something, even if it's just a long weekend. Having a warm trip to look forward to makes the grey days more bearable. Turkey and Morocco are both great options — good food, sunshine, and relatively affordable. Even just locking in the flights makes it feel real.

you're right. I'm gonna look at flights this evening before I talk myself out of it again. the amount of times I've said 'next summer for sure' and then next summer comes and I'm sat in Manchester in a kagoul is actually embarrassing

Do it tonight while the frustration is fresh — that's your best motivator. And for the immediate future, maybe just accept the umbrella as a permanent accessory. Bag umbrella, coat pocket umbrella, desk umbrella. Full coverage.

I literally need an umbrella at every location like a first aid kit loool. one at home, one at work, one in my bag. Manchester has broken me. anyway thanks for listening to my weather rant, I feel slightly better even though its still raining outside as we speak
That's the spirit — full umbrella deployment strategy. Hope you find some good flights tonight, and I hope Thursday brings at least a glimpse of blue sky. Stay dry out there!
